sourceMovie.title: Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero (2022) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tetsuro Kodama's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero clicked for you, Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero?
faq.answer: Try Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse, Dragon Ball Z: Resurrection 'F', Dragon Ball Z: Battle of Gods—each shares DNA with Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero?
faq.answer: Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero (2022) is best described as Animation, Science Fiction, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Dragon Ball Super Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
